perturbation.original_text: (v) During the Term and during the sell-off period, MusclePharm shall make royalty payments in U.S. dollars for the respective quarters ending on the last day of September, December, March and June (each, a "Royalty Period") within thirty (30) days from the end of each quarterly period. Each such royalty payment shall include an itemized statement showing the nature and source of such royalties, including (i) the number of units of Licensed Products sold (by country and customer); (ii) the total number of units returned for which credit was given and the total dollar amount of such credits, and (iii) the total gross sales and the total royalties due with respect to such gross sales, and each itemized statement shall be certified by a duly authorized officer of MusclePharm. Such statements shall be in the form attached hereto as Exhibit "B" and furnished to Lender whether or not any Licensed Products were sold during the Royalty Period.
perturbation.changed_text: (v) During the Term and during the sell-off period, MusclePharm shall make royalty payments in U.S. dollars for the respective quarters ending on the last day of September, December, March and June (each, a "Royalty Period") within sixty (60) days from the end of each quarterly period. Each such royalty payment shall include an itemized statement showing the nature and source of such royalties, including (i) the number of units of Licensed Products sold (by country and customer); (ii) the total number of units returned for which credit was given and the total dollar amount of such credits, and (iii) the total gross sales and the total royalties due with respect to such gross sales, and each itemized statement shall be certified by a duly authorized officer of MusclePharm. Such statements shall be in the form attached hereto as Exhibit "B" and furnished to Lender whether or not any Licensed Products were sold during the Royalty Period.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' for royalty payments after the end of each quarterly period creates an in-text contradiction with Exhibit C Section (1), Section (2) and Section (3), where the payment is due on July 23, October 1 and February 1 of each year, independently of the payment being 30 or 60 days of the ending of each quarterly period. This creates uncertainty about when the payments are due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit "C" Section (1) Guaranteed Minimum Royalty during the initial Term: Contract Year Minimum Royalty Timing of Payment One $1,500,000 $500,000 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2013; October 1, 2013; February 1, 2014 Two $2,000,000 $666,666.66 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2014; October 1, 2014; February 1, 2015 Three $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2015; October 1, 2015; February 1, 2016
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 'thirty (30) days' to 'sixty (60) days' after the end of each quarter. However, Exhibit C specifies fixed payment dates (July 23, October 1, February 1), independent of any quarterly period. This creates a direct conflict, as one section mandates payment 30/60 days after the quarter, while the other specifies absolute dates. This conflict creates uncertainty and thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Exhibit "C" Section (1) Guaranteed Minimum Royalty during the initial Term: Contract Year Minimum Royalty Timing of Payment One $1,500,000 $500,000 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2013; October 1, 2013; February 1, 2014 Two $2,000,000 $666,666.66 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2014; October 1, 2014; February 1, 2015 Three $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2015; October 1, 2015; February 1, 2016
perturbation.changed_text: Exhibit "C" Section (1) Guaranteed Minimum Royalty during the initial Term: Contract Year Minimum Royalty Timing of Payment One $1,500,000 $500,000 payment due on the following dates: August 23, 2013; October 1, 2013; February 1, 2014 Two $2,000,000 $666,666.66 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2014; October 1, 2014; February 1, 2015 Three $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2015; October 1, 2015; February 1, 2016
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment date from 'July 23, 2013' to 'August 23, 2013' in Exhibit C Section (1) creates an in-text contradiction with section 1(a), which states 'The Term shall commence on July 23, 2013'. If the initial payment date is August 23, the agreement lacks clarity on payments for the first month, July 23 - August 23. It is unclear how the payments would be calculated for this period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Term: (a) This Agreement shall have an Initial Term of three (3) years. The Term shall commence on July 23, 2013 and shall expire on July 22, 2016, unless otherwise terminated earlier pursuant to Section 9 of this Agreement. The period from July 23, 2013 to July 22, 2014 shall be referred to as the "First Contract Year". The period from July 23, 2014 to July 22, 2015 shall be referred to as the "Second Contract Year". The period from July 23, 2015 to July 22, 2016 shall be referred to as the "Third Contract Year".
perturbation.justification: The change to the payment date in Exhibit C, Section 1 from July 23, 2013 to August 23, 2013 directly contradicts Section 1(a) of the agreement. Section 1(a) explicitly states that 'The Term shall commence on July 23, 2013'. Changing the initial payment date to August 23 introduces an inconsistency regarding payments due for the period between July 23, 2013 and August 23, 2013, creating a contradiction in when the first payment is due, according to the definition of inconsistencies. The payment schedule no longer aligns with the contract start date.

perturbation.original_text: (viii) MusclePharm's obligations for the payment of a Royalty and the Guaranteed Minimum Royalty (as defined below) shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and will continue for so long as MusclePharm continues to manufacture, sell or otherwise market the Licensed Products. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Lender shall be entitled to receive a guaranteed minimum royalty for each Contract Year including the Additional Term, if any (the "Guaranteed Minimum Royalty"), payable in accordance with Exhibit "C" attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (viii) MusclePharm's obligations for the payment of a Royalty and the Guaranteed Minimum Royalty (as defined below) shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and will continue for so long as MusclePharm continues to manufacture, sell or otherwise market the Licensed Products. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Lender shall be entitled to receive a guaranteed minimum royalty for each Contract Year but not including any Additional Term (the "Guaranteed Minimum Royalty"), payable in accordance with Exhibit "C" attached hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'including the Additional Term, if any' from the definition of 'Guaranteed Minimum Royalty' creates an in-text contradiction with Exhibit C Sections (2) and (3), which specify guaranteed minimum royalties during the First and Second Additional Terms. The change creates ambiguity about whether the guaranteed minimum royalties apply during any additional terms or only during the initial term, creating a legal uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section (2) Guaranteed Minimum Royalty during the First Additional Term: In the event that the Renewal Threshold is achieved in the Third Contract Year, during the First Additional Term the Minimum Royalty and Timing of Payment shall be as follows: Contract Year Minimum Royalty Timing of Payment Four $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2016; October 1, 2016; February 1, 2017 Five $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2017; October 1, 2017; February 1, 2018 Six $2,500,000 $833,333.33 payment due on the following dates: July 23, 2018; October 1, 2018; February 1, 2019
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Guaranteed Minimum Royalty applied 'including the Additional Term, if any'. The changed text explicitly excludes 'any Additional Term' from the Guaranteed Minimum Royalty. This directly contradicts Exhibit C Sections (2) and (3) which specify minimum royalty payments during the First and Second Additional Terms. The removal of the additional term from the definition creates a clear conflict, creating ambiguity about payment obligations during the Additional Term, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable regarding royalty payments during any renewal term.
